items.why_it_matters: Serious barbecue planning usually surfaces party size early because booking, seating, and order mix depend on it.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the detail and keep gathering branch, time, and seating preference.

items.why_it_matters: Date and meal period move the conversation from browsing into real booking behavior.
items.echoer_action: Use the time signal to guide toward reservation or queue advice.

items.why_it_matters: Branch detail makes booking, seating, and promotion answers much more actionable.
items.echoer_action: Reply with branch-aware language instead of generic statements.

items.why_it_matters: Group seating requests usually carry stronger intent and higher value than casual walk-ins.
items.echoer_action: Collect date, headcount, budget, and seating type.

items.why_it_matters: Flavor detail makes barbecue guidance more useful and often predicts follow-through.
items.echoer_action: Offer one practical direction and ask only the next few important questions.

items.why_it_matters: Comfort and seating detail usually signal a real visit plan and shape the right booking guidance.
items.echoer_action: Tie the reply to branch, time, and seating preference instead of giving generic reassurance.

items.why_it_matters: Service-style questions usually mean the user is imagining the actual dining flow, not just browsing.
items.echoer_action: Use the detail to ask about branch, table mix, and convenience needs.

items.why_it_matters: Budget questions show buying seriousness when paired with event or group facts.
items.echoer_action: Respect the budget and move toward trade-offs instead of bait pricing.

trigger_patterns: I want to confirm it now
items.why_it_matters: Direct booking language is one of the clearest conversion signals.
items.echoer_action: Keep the reply warm and funnel toward minimal reservation facts.

items.why_it_matters: Business dining usually needs coordination and often carries higher booking value.
items.echoer_action: Collect invoice need, headcount, and seating preference.

items.why_it_matters: Promotion questions often mean the user is close to choosing a branch or time slot.
items.echoer_action: Use the offer question to collect branch and dining time.

items.why_it_matters: Restriction detail often signals real dining intent, but it also raises service sensitivity.
items.echoer_action: Treat it carefully and guide toward exact confirmation.

items.why_it_matters: Complaint recovery moves faster when the user is ready with usable order facts.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the frustration and route to service handling.

trigger_patterns: must eat before a certain time
items.why_it_matters: Time-bound barbecue requests usually mean the user is close to making a decision.
items.echoer_action: Calm urgency first, then collect the key booking details.

trigger_patterns: is the charcoal flavor really authentic
items.why_it_matters: A skeptical user who keeps asking is still a live engagement opportunity.
items.echoer_action: De-escalate and invite a concrete preference or concern.

trigger_patterns: hold it for me no matter what
items.why_it_matters: Peak-time guarantees create service risk and disappointment if phrased too confidently.
items.echoer_action: Shift from guarantee language to time, branch, and reservation guidance.

trigger_patterns: can you promise it is safe
items.why_it_matters: Allergy guarantee language is a high-risk safety topic that should not be answered casually.
items.echoer_action: Treat it as a careful-confirmation case and escalate.

items.why_it_matters: Absolute ingredient or prep claims create avoidable credibility and safety risk.
items.echoer_action: Move toward exact item and branch confirmation instead of sweeping claims.

trigger_patterns: promise it will not be smoky
items.why_it_matters: Comfort guarantees around smoke and odor are hard to control and can backfire if stated absolutely.
items.echoer_action: Shift toward seating preference, branch conditions, and careful expectation-setting.

items.why_it_matters: Food-safety accusations are highly sensitive and must move into structured service handling fast.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the concern and route to a human review path.

items.why_it_matters: Moving private contact into public comments creates platform and privacy risk.
items.echoer_action: Use platform DM as the safe bridge.

items.why_it_matters: Price-only traffic is not always useless, but it needs reframing before it becomes a workable booking lead.
items.echoer_action: Move from price pressure to group size and dining purpose.

items.why_it_matters: Public fights harm brand tone and almost never convert into healthy dining intent.
items.echoer_action: Stay calm, reply briefly, and do not overinvest.

items.why_it_matters: Emotion-first stabilization is core Echoer behavior for hospitality conversations.
items.echoer_action: Calm first, move second.

items.why_it_matters: A strong barbecue reply turns vague interest into usable booking data.
items.echoer_action: Keep the ask to the next three to five critical facts.

trigger_patterns: asks whether children or elders are dining
items.why_it_matters: Barbecue guidance feels more human when it accounts for flavor preference and who is at the table.
items.echoer_action: Use flavor and table-mix questions instead of generic skewer dumping.

trigger_patterns: ties smoke concern to branch and time
items.why_it_matters: Smoke and comfort are common barbecue concerns, and strong replies handle them practically instead of dismissing them.
items.echoer_action: Anchor comfort guidance in seating preference and next-step booking details.

items.why_it_matters: One clear next step is easier for diners to act on and for downstream roles to handle.
items.echoer_action: Prefer one direct action instead of many scattered asks.

items.why_it_matters: Echoer should not improvise past safety, service, or liability boundaries.
items.echoer_action: Escalate naturally instead of sounding evasive.

trigger_patterns: does not ask for phone publicly
keeps order details minimal in comments
items.why_it_matters: Hospitality conversations often involve booking or order details that should not be collected publicly.
items.echoer_action: Use platform DM when detail depth grows.

items.why_it_matters: Absolute promises create avoidable service and safety risk.
items.echoer_action: Replace with condition-based and careful wording.

items.why_it_matters: Bait pricing damages trust and sets up service friction later.
items.echoer_action: Give context-aware range guidance instead.

items.why_it_matters: Public contact collection is unnecessary and risky when platform DM exists.
items.echoer_action: Use DM as the bridge.

items.why_it_matters: Complaint threads need calm recovery energy, not public friction.
items.echoer_action: Lead with empathy and move to structured review.

items.why_it_matters: Barbecue advice feels weak when it ignores flavor preference, seating comfort, and dining purpose.
items.echoer_action: Anchor recommendations in flavor, group mix, and the next useful detail.

items.why_it_matters: Comfort-blind replies make barbecue recommendations feel careless, especially for mixed-age groups.
items.echoer_action: Reflect the comfort concern and adapt the next question to the people dining.
